__fixture__.name: reconstructed_ai_provider_policy_decision
__fixture__.scenario: A managed AI provider route is disabled by a signed emergency record. The deny decision can be reconstructed locally from bundle history, emergency-disable metadata, and effective configuration sources even when the hosted admin console is unavailable.

exercised_axes.local_reconstruction: complete from cache history and source chain

tenant_scope.scope_note: Tenant and workspace are export-safe opaque refs.

payload_digests.redaction_note: Policy bundle digest is exported without raw rule bodies.
Emergency-disable digest is exported without raw signatures or incident payload.

signature_metadata.verification_note: Policy bundle and emergency-disable payload verified under the current emergency trust root.

refresh_history.summary: The managed push activated a signed policy bundle carrying an emergency provider disable.

last_known_good.local_core_posture: Local editing, local models, and BYOK routes remain available where separately configured.
last_known_good.managed_action_posture: The disabled managed provider route remains blocked for the session and until signed successor policy clears it.
last_known_good.summary: The previous bundle remains recorded as history but does not override the active emergency disable.

emergency_disable_records.summary: The emergency record disables the managed AI provider route and does not silently re-enable during the session.

effective_configuration_sources.value_projection: Managed provider route is available when configured.
effective_configuration_sources.summary: The default admits the route only as a configured capability.

effective_configuration_sources.value_projection: Provider route is allowed for managed workspaces unless a higher emergency record fires.
effective_configuration_sources.summary: The active admin bundle alone would admit the managed provider route.

effective_configuration_sources.value_projection: Workspace selected the managed provider route.
effective_configuration_sources.summary: The workspace value is a candidate only inside the policy ceiling.

effective_configuration_sources.value_projection: Emergency provider-disable ratchet blocks the route.
effective_configuration_sources.summary: Emergency disable has highest precedence and forces the provider route off.

local_reconstruction.proof_note: The local cache contains the active epoch, source chain, emergency-disable record, and refresh receipt needed to reconstruct the deny decision.

tenant_scope.scope_note: The export includes opaque scope refs only.

vendor_console_handoff_notes: Hosted console may include incident handling details, but the local artifact explains why the provider route is disabled.
browser_handoff_notes: Browser handoff opens only an opaque admin route ref; it is not required for local reconstruction.
export_explainability.summary: The export carries enough signed metadata to reconstruct the deny decision without exposing incident payloads.

notes: Emergency-disable precedence overrides a workspace-selected provider route and must not silently clear on restart.
